response:
If the complex number $ z $ satisfies $ iz = \sqrt{3} - i $, then $ |z| $ can be found by first solving for $ z $. Multiplying both sides of the equation by $ -i $ gives $ z = -i(\sqrt{3} - i) = -i\sqrt{3} + i^2 = -i\sqrt{3} - 1 $. The modulus of $ z $ is then $ |z| = \sqrt{(-1)^2 + (-\sqrt{3})^2} = \sqrt{1 + 3} = \sqrt{4} = 2 $.  

The value of $ |z| $ is 2.
